channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Don’t Fret INTERVIEW: A Horror Game That Should be Music to Your Ears
item.content:encoded:
A horror game where you play as a guitar may sound odd on the surface, and it’s even odder when you actually play it. In my Summer Game Fest 2026 demo, I quickly learned that Don’t Fret is a finely tuned game with scares and notable influences. All of which are discussed in my special interview with the game’s creative director and producer.

Don’t Fret is a play on words as you take control of Fret, a literal guitar trapped in a musical school nightmare. It has that cutesy Five Nights at Freddy’s and Poppy Playtime vibe that makes its actual contents even more unsettling, from faceless enemies to other monstrosities I won’t spoil.

I had the privilege of speaking to Scary Kid Studios owner Russell McKamey (and met co-creator Ryan Giordano) at Summer Game Fest 2026, shortly after playing Don’t Fret, to see how on earth he and the team concocted this symphony of strangeness.

‘Silent Hill and Resident Evil Are Big Inspirations’

My first question had to be answered: ‘ Why a guitar for the main character?’

The response:

‘My first instrument was a guitar, so growing up, I learned to play. It’s a really challenging instrument, like most things from a design standpoint. I think that the guitar lent itself to the character design more than other instruments might. Yeah, you have like a flute or a trumpet or a piano, and I did feel like that guitar is probably the most universal of instruments, where just I don’t know, it’s a cozy instrument, you know, felt like it felt like a younger kid could be a guitar, right?’

This told me everything I needed to know about Don’t Fret and the vision here.

You can’t help but love passion projects, something with emotional and historical attachment. It makes even more sense when I realised that Russell is also the co-owner of the Rockit Music YouTube channel—specializing in nerdcore songs, with an audience of 1.5 million.

Music is a central mechanism and plot device in Don’t Fret, and I learned a little more about how big of an impact it has on the game:

‘A lot of games focus on just jump scares, and the cheap thrill, and we want to focus on storytelling, building up tension, and making these really cinematically cool moments.

It’s not a rhythm horror game, you don’t need to know music, but music is embedded in everything: In every puzzle and in every core aspect of the mechanics of the game, you see music everywhere, and so I think that in that regard it’s one of the most unique things in terms of like a horror game that’s out there.’

From my own experience, one second I was hiding from the clutches of a monster, unable to fight back as I was only armed with a Guitar Tuner that simply distracts rather than devastates, the next I was thrown into a desperate escape sequence with a thumping track that briefly made me think I was in a Blade nightclub.

Russell explained how you upgrade the Guitar Tuner as you progress, but this feels very much like an isolated horror game where fighting back isn’t nearly as effective as hiding and keeping quiet—again, playing into our sound and music themes.

I expected the usual hide-and-seek motif you get with this brand of horror game, like an Outlast, for example, but I was caught off guard by a couple of puzzles that made me think twice. One required me to flick back and forth between several notes to accurately play a certain jingle, all the while being watched by a faceless teacher that still gives me the heebie jeebies even now.

Interestingly, some puzzles will be randomized, some won’t, and this should add to the game’s replayability. The five-person-strong development team has managed to whip up a complete adventure of roughly ‘six to eight hours,’ and there will be some DLC in the future.

If you’re wondering if this is for you, then talk of other famous horror games could lure you in further:

‘Silent Hill and Resident Evil are big inspirations, I think. We went after this to try to have a visual aesthetic that was reminiscent of a Bioshock, but on the surface, it looked kind of like a nice game, but when you dive into it, it’s got a really gnarly story. It starts to get darker and darker as the game goes on, and there are really nasty elements.’

Russell claims it’s an ‘8/10’ on the Scaro-o-meter scale, and I can definitely see that. Don’t Fret is a visually striking and audibly enchanting experience, and you can check out its release date trailer below.

There’s also a free demo on its Steam page.

The Insider Gaming Discord is the perfect place to let me know what you think about Don’t Fret, and if its sound is cool enough for you.

We also have SGF 2026 previews on Turok Origins, Clutch, Blood Message, Valor Mortis, Escape Academy 2, and Saw: Genesis.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

item.title: Resident Evil 0 Remake Was ‘Rebooted Mid-Dev’, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A recent claim suggests that Resident Evil 0 Remake was rebooted during development, with a new team now handling it.

There have been longstanding rumors about the development of a Resident Evil 0 Remake, alongside discussions about a Code Veronica Remake. While one of these games has been officially revealed and players are eagerly anticipating its release, the development of the 0 Remake appears to have encountered some challenges. Fortunately, it is now in capable hands.

The claims about a mid-development reboot for Resident Evil 0 Remake come from the latest post on X (formerly Twitter) by Dusk Golem. The insider recently replied to a comment about 0 Remake being handled by the Resident Evil 3 Remake team, stating that Resident Evil 0 remake was “rebooted mid-dev”. After the reboot, the remake is being led by ‘Capcom Div 1″.

Jun Takeuchi leads Capcom Division 1, the company’s flagship development team, which handles mainline AAA entries from series such as Resident Evil, Devil May Cry, Dragon’s Dogma, and more. So fans will be happy to know that the main team at Capcom is handling development of this remake.

Resident Evil 0 was originally released in 2002 and received a remastered version in 2016. This game acts as a prequel to the first Resident Evil title, taking players back to 1998 to investigate a series of unusual murders on the outskirts of Raccoon City. Players take on the roles of two characters: rookie cop Rebecca Chambers and death row convict, ex-Navy lieutenant Billy Coen. Together, they uncover the truth behind the events that led to the horrors of the original game set in the Mansion.

In other news, previous rumors suggest that Resident Evil: Veronica arrives in 2027, with 2028 reserved for the Zero Remake. Additionally, Resident Evil Requiem’s story DLC is further away than players thought. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil 0 remake being rebooted mid-dev? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the merit of Fallout New Vegas remake rumors. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fallout New Vegas Remake Rumors ‘Have Merit’, Insider Claims
item.content:encoded:
An insider has revealed that the rumors about Fallout New Vegas Remake are credible.

There was a report earlier this year about the future of XBOX that mentioned that remakes of Fallout 3 and New Vegas were in the works. The words ‘New Vegas 2’ were also recently uttered by a Fallout writer, giving fans more hope for it. It seems that, according to another insider, these claims are real.

Fallout New Vegas Remake Seems to be In Development

The new claim that the Fallout New Vegas Remake is real comes from a reliable insider, NateTheHate. In one X thread (formerly Twitter), the insider was asked whether the rumors about a New Vegas remake were true. To which Nate replied, stating, “The previous rumors have merit.” The insider didn’t provide any additional information, such as release timelines or how they are treating the upcoming title, but this information is enough to give players more hope.

Fallout: New Vegas was released in 2010 and offers an expansive open world set in the Mojave Wasteland, featuring iconic locations such as the Hoover Dam and the Las Vegas Strip. In this game, players interact with a diverse cast of intriguing characters, confront power-hungry factions, and utilize special weapons to defeat mutated enemies. This title serves as a sequel to the critically acclaimed Fallout 3.

In other news, Fallout 76 is finally getting a long-awaited feature with new ‘CAMPanions’ that join you on your adventures in the wasteland. Additionally, Insider Gaming recently sat down with Bethesda Game Studios to talk about Fallout 76 and the future of PvP, and the PAST of Nuclear Winter. What are your thoughts on rumors about Fallout New Vegas Remake having merit? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the XBOX losing ‘hundreds’ on each console sale. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: G2 Extend Hans Sama LEC Contract Through to 2028
item.content:encoded:
G2 Esports have announced a two-year contract extension for its League of Legends pro Steven ‘Hans Sama’ Liv.

Hans Sama’s contract was due to run out after LoL Worlds this year, but will now last through to the end of the 2028 season.

The League of Legends pro recently helped the team to its 19th domestic title after winning the Spring Split Playoffs, the ninth of his career.

He is the second G2 player to extend their contracts to 2028, after G2 offered jungler Rudy ‘SkewMond’ Semaan a new contract back in February.

Hans Sama has established himself as one of the strongest ADCs in Europe, competing in his ninth professional season.

Which is why there was so much surprise when Team France’s Esports Nations Cup LoL roster didn’t include him.

France’s coach, Quentin ‘Zeph’ Viguié, opted for his Karmine Corp player Caliste ‘Caliste’ Henry-Hennebert.

So far this year, Hans Sama has continued a strong duo with his support, Lampros ‘Labrov’ Papoutsakis.

The duo helped G2’s phenomenal First Stand run, sweeping both LCK teams Gen.G and BNK FEARX.

Now, G2 are headed into LoL MSI as EMEA’s first seeds and will be looking to go one further than their second-place finish at First Stand.

Hans Sama’s Impressive Career

Hans began competing at the top level in 2017 after helping Misfits Gaming earn promotion into the EU LCS.

That year, they qualified for Worlds and infamously pushed SKT T1 to five games and just narrowly fell short of a major upset.

He then had a long wait for his first trophy and had to move to North America for it, winning the LCS Lock In 2020 with Team Liquid.

G2 then brought Hans back to Europe in 2023 and have won numerous LEC trophies together since.

Is Hans Sama currently the strongest Western ADC? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord. 

Meanwhile, G2 legend Luka ‘Perkz’ Perković is back with the team in a coaching role.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Escape Academy 2: Back 2 School PREVIEW: Still The Best Escape Room Series
item.content:encoded:
I love escape room games. Board games. In-person setups. Apps. It doesn’t matter. You can’t beat a critical test of your logical thinking and on-the-fly deduction skills. Escape Academy arrived on the scene in 2022, and it took a while for me and my fiancée to find it, but when we did, we had the best time. Eascape Academy 2 looks like more of the same and then some.

A good escape room game requires you to be attentive to the entire location, note down crucial numbers and names, and have you clawing at your own scalp in abject confusion and frustration. Escape Academy 2 has plenty of these moments, and like a good puzzle, the immense feeling of satisfaction when you finally uncover the solution is comparable only to besting a Soulslike boss.

The Ultimate Escape From Reality

Alongside fellow Insider Gaming Senior Editor “A2TheP” Albert Perkins, AP, and I were a whirlwind tour-de-force of puzzle-solving power…kind of. We took time to create some chemistry between us—ironic, as the first few puzzles were in the Science Wing of the academy—but we eventually started meshing.

With no help at all (we needed a couple of hints to get us going), we started finding clues, putting the pieces together, and our first room was a wonderful intertwining test. We needed to find animal pieces, have basic knowledge of the animal kingdom, and be observant of birds.

The overall challenge was compartmentalized into mini-puzzles, but you needed to refer back to finished solutions to help you with the remaining brainteasers.

This is why the Escape Academy franchise is above The Talos Principle and The Witness for my money.

These FEEL like proper escape rooms I’d pay good money for in RL. You need to seek out every square inch of the room, speak to everyone involved, and use a basic grasp of common sense and pragmatism to figure out the solutions. We sampled several additional rooms and began to feel smarter with every room—a false sense of security, I’m sure, with later puzzles sure to ramp up the difficulty.

Take Your Mind For A Walk

The basic premise of Escape Academy 2 is still familiar: You need to solve isolated puzzles and find solutions in one room. However, this time, you’re free to explore the academy from the get-go, and we’ve moved away from the linear, rigid structure of solving one room (or level) before moving on to the next.

At one point, we needed money to purchase the materials for the next puzzle. We did this by finding hidden money throughout the school campus and by completing side jobs: One of many new conclusions in the sequel.

They’re essentially smaller puzzles with a fun thematic story beat attached. Once we collected enough money, we headed back to the store, bought the materials…and our demo ended. I was devastated. My brain wasn’t ready to sign off. But you can see the length and scope of Escape Academy 2, and how it fixes the issues with the first game.

Exploration is key, and you’re encouraged to interact with the title’s many NPCs and recurring characters.

My only downside with the demo is that the performance wasn’t the best, but this is an early build of the game, with the full release sure to be smooth as butter.

Whereas the original could be completed in one or two sessions, I was informed at Summer Game Fest that you can expect closer to 15 hours for Escape Academy!

This is a bigger and better sequel in every sense of the word.

Sign up for the Insider Gaming Discord to talk with us about Escape Academy and your love of escape rooms in general!

We also have SGF 2026 previews on Turok Origins, Clutch, Blood Message, Valor Mortis, and Saw: Genesis.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: XBOX CSO Clarifies Stance on In-Game Advertising
item.content:encoded:
XBOX Chief Strategy Officer Matthew Ball has clarified his stance on in-game advertising and his recent comments regarding the ad-supported approach adopted by Netflix and other streamers in the entertainment industry.

The streaming world of Netflix, Disney+, and other heavy hitters once offered a more premium vision of television without commercials and with full autonomy over what you watch and when. While the on-demand aspect remains the primary driver of streaming’s upside, many platforms now offer an ad-supported tier that serves as an entry point for consumers who prefer to pay less.

The largest gaming platform to adopt a subscription model is Xbox Game Pass. Recently, Ball mentioned that he believes the potential for advertising in gaming could help offset rising game development costs without raising hardware or software prices or resorting to a microtransaction model.

XBOX Exec Matthew Ball Explains His Stance on Ads in Video Games

During a recent interview with Christopher Dring of The Game Business, Matthew Ball pointed to the TV streaming world as the best example of how an ad-supported experience offers alternatives without taking away from those who want the ad-free experience

“I think the best evidence comes from the [TV] streaming space. In excess of 100% of net adds in the United States for years and years have been on the ad-supported tier. That has not excluded anyone from ad-free experiences. Those products are still there. They’re still popular,” he said.

“I don’t have anything to report on Microsoft’s plans. I’ve frankly not read into them despite my interest in the category. But it is coming from that perspective of how do we reach more people? How do we keep our products affordable?”

When clarifying a tweet about his remarks claiming XBOX was considering ad-supported gaming, he noted he did not say that, but he personally believes that interrupting the gameplay experience with ads would be a bad move.

Ball also recently commented on the future of Project Helix and how XBOX is reevaluating its approach to the next generation of gaming. Read those comments here.

What do you think of Matthew Ball’s comments concerning ads in gaming? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: XBOX Losing ‘Hundreds’ on Each Console Sale, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A recent claim states that Microsoft is losing hundreds of dollars on each XBOX Series X/S sold.

There has been a lot of talk about XBOX consoles recently, with higher-ups already discussing a reevaluation of the next-generation console amid cost concerns. However, the company has also reaffirmed its desire to remain on consoles. But it seems there is a current situation with console sales in which the company is suffering losses.

XBOX is Suffering Major Losses on XBOX Series X/S Sales

A report from Windows Central has revealed that XBOX is experiencing significant losses in console sales. It appears that XBOX has struggled to secure sufficient memory at fixed prices, preventing it from maintaining positive profit margins. According to information provided to Jez Corden, Xbox is currently losing “not dozens, but hundreds of dollars” on each XBOX Series X|S console sold. Additionally, CEO Asha Sharma has noted that memory prices have risen by 700% since the XBOX Series X/S pricing was set.

Memory scarcity issues are impacting not only XBOX but all the major players in the industry. The CEO of XBOX has already addressed how this shortage is affecting the pricing and availability of their next-generation console. Nintendo has had to reconsider pricing for the Switch 2, while the PlayStation 5’s price has increased. Additionally, Valve is reassessing the pricing of its upcoming Steam Machine. As a result, Xbox will soon need to take action to mitigate potential losses.

In other news, Microsoft reportedly relied on Call of Duty profits to support XBOX, but an off-year for the franchise disrupted growth plans and revenue. Additionally, XBOX Chief Strategy Officer Matthew Ball has commented on the brand’s approach to exclusivity. What are your thoughts on XBOX losing hundreds of dollars on each console sale? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the STALKER 2 Cost of Hope trailer, revealing new characters. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Brazilian Woman Wins $500,000 Playing Candy Crush Saga
item.content:encoded:
The Candy Crush All Stars tournament has wrapped, and standing at the top of the table over millions of competitors stands a Brazilian woman named Luana. At the live final in London, ten contestants took to the stage to fight for the lion’s share of a massive $1 million by playing what might seem like one of the unlikeliest games imaginable.

Luana, the champion from Bahia, Brazil, walks away with a $500,000 cut and a one-of-a-kind championship ring, adorned with sapphires, rubies, and emeralds.

Mobile Gaming Wins Again

Candy Crush is often dismissed as being as casual a game as you could think of, but it just won an unassuming player from Brazil a whopping $500,000. The Candy Crush All Stars tournament represents the pinnacle of the esports scene in the space and drew the attention of thousands of viewers.

In a statement, Luana explained:

I’ve been playing for as long as I can remember. I used to be really imaginative. I’ve dreamt about competing in live tournaments, picture a host commentating on my moves, imagine the venue, the opponents.

The fact that ‘Candy Crush All Stars’ exists and I got to compete in it feels like it came straight out of my own imagination. I feel like I’m honouring a younger version of myself by literally living her dreams.

You can check out Luana’s winning moment here:

Mobile esports are immensely powerful, pulling in millions of fans with each successive event. It’s no big secret that mobile gaming makes up the most densely populated and valuable vertical in the industry, but the mobile esports space is also growing at a staggering pace.

Tournaments like the Candy Crush All Stars are a testament to that fact.

Do you think you’re good enough at Candy Crush to win a $500,000 prize? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Blizzard has revealed Shion’s new abilities

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
